---
title: "scRNA_DE"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-08"
---

## loading libraries
```{r libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(DESeq2)
```

```{r deseq object}
#load RNA data with ReadMtx function from Seurat package
counts_matrix <- ReadMtx(
  "/home/fgsasse_lrs_1/Downloads/BA/BA_data/Pseudobulks/RNA/celltypes/matrix.mtx",
  "/home/fgsasse_lrs_1/Downloads/BA/BA_data/Pseudobulks/RNA/celltypes/barcodes.tsv",
  "/home/fgsasse_lrs_1/Downloads/BA/BA_data/Pseudobulks/RNA/celltypes/features.tsv",
  cell.column = 1,
  feature.column = 1,
  cell.sep = "\t",
  feature.sep = "\t",
  skip.cell = 0,
  skip.feature = 0,
  mtx.transpose = TRUE,
  unique.features = TRUE,
  strip.suffix = FALSE
)


dss <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
          countData = counts_matrix,
          colData = colData,
          design = ~ celltype
)
seu.obj <- (counts = counts_matrix)

```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
